       kglite <COMMAND>

For more information, try '--help'.

error: the following required arguments were not provided:
  <GRAPH>
  <QUERY>

For more information, try '--help'.

unknown_subcommand.stderr: note: unknown-command does not exist — starting an empty in-memory graph

help.describe:
Print the XML graph description used by agents for structure discovery

Usage: kglite describe [OPTIONS] <GRAPH>

Arguments:
  <GRAPH>  Path to the `.kgl` file

Options:
      --types <TYPES>
          Comma-separated node types for focused detail
      --type-search <TYPE_SEARCH>
          Search node types by name
      --connections
          Include connection overview
      --connection-types <CONNECTION_TYPES>
          Comma-separated connection types for deep-dive detail
      --cypher
          Include compact Cypher reference
      --cypher-topics <CYPHER_TOPICS>
          Comma-separated Cypher topics for detailed docs
      --fluent
          Include compact fluent API reference
      --fluent-topics <FLUENT_TOPICS>
          Comma-separated fluent API topics for detailed docs
      --max-pairs <MAX_PAIRS>
          Max `(source_type, target_type)` pairs for connection deep-dives
      --sample-truncate <SAMPLE_TRUNCATE>
          Truncate long sample strings to this many characters [default: 40]
  -h, --help
          Print help
help.diff:
Show what changed between two `.kgl` graphs — a structural delta over the deterministic text projection: `-` lines dropped from A, `+` lines added in B (a node/edge whose properties changed shows as a `-`/`+` pair)

Usage: kglite diff <A> <B>

Arguments:
  <A>  The "before" `.kgl`
  <B>  The "after" `.kgl`

Options:
  -h, --help  Print help
help.export-text:
Print a deterministic, human-readable text projection of a `.kgl` to stdout — the canonical form for a git `textconv` diff filter. Set up: `git config diff.kglite.textconv "kglite export-text"` + `echo '*.kgl diff=kglite' >> .gitattributes`

Usage: kglite export-text <FILE>

Arguments:
  <FILE>  Path to the `.kgl` file

Options:
  -h, --help  Print help
help.query:
Run a read-only Cypher query against a `.kgl` graph and print the result

Usage: kglite query [OPTIONS] <GRAPH> <QUERY>

Arguments:
  <GRAPH>  Path to the `.kgl` file
  <QUERY>  Cypher query string

Options:
      --format <FORMAT>  Output format [default: table] [possible values: table, csv, json]
  -h, --help             Print help
help.ready-set:
Print the dependency frontier from `CALL ready_set(...)`

Usage: kglite ready-set [OPTIONS] --done <DONE> <GRAPH>

Arguments:
  <GRAPH>  Path to the `.kgl` file

Options:
      --relationship <RELATIONSHIP>  Dependency relationship type [default: DEPENDS_ON]
      --done <DONE>                  Done predicate over `n`, for example: `n.status = "done"`
      --node-type <NODE_TYPE>        Optional node type to include in the frontier
      --format <FORMAT>              Output format [default: table] [possible values: table, csv, json]
  -h, --help                         Print help
help.root:
Interactive Cypher shell for kglite knowledge graphs — the sqlite3-style REPL: `kglite app.kgl` opens a prompt that runs Cypher and dot-commands (.labels, .schema, .dump, .read, .mode) against a single .kgl file. Pure-Rust single binary, no libpython.

Usage: kglite [GRAPH]
       kglite <COMMAND>

Commands:
  query        Run a read-only Cypher query against a `.kgl` graph and print the result
  write        Run a write-capable Cypher statement against a `.kgl` graph
  ready-set    Print the dependency frontier from `CALL ready_set(...)`
  describe     Print the XML graph description used by agents for structure discovery
  session      Keep one graph loaded and process JSONL requests on stdin
  export-text  Print a deterministic, human-readable text projection of a `.kgl` to stdout — the canonical form for a git `textconv` diff filter. Set up: `git config diff.kglite.textconv "kglite export-text"` + `echo '*.kgl diff=kglite' >> .gitattributes`
  diff         Show what changed between two `.kgl` graphs — a structural delta over the deterministic text projection: `-` lines dropped from A, `+` lines added in B (a node/edge whose properties changed shows as a `-`/`+` pair)
  help         Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Arguments:
  [GRAPH]  Path to a `.kgl` file to open. If omitted (or the file does not exist yet), the shell starts with a fresh in-memory graph

Options:
  -h, --help     Print help
  -V, --version  Print version
help.session:
Keep one graph loaded and process JSONL requests on stdin

Usage: kglite session [OPTIONS] <GRAPH>

Arguments:
  <GRAPH>  Path to the `.kgl` file

Options:
      --format <FORMAT>            Default output format for query/write responses [default: json] [possible values: table, csv, json]
      --save-on-exit               Save the graph when the session exits successfully
      --write-scope <WRITE_SCOPE>  Comma-separated node-type whitelist for write requests
      --git-sha <GIT_SHA>          Git SHA to stamp on auto_timestamp types for write requests
      --modified-by <MODIFIED_BY>  Actor id to stamp on auto_timestamp types for write requests
  -h, --help                       Print help
help.write:
Run a write-capable Cypher statement against a `.kgl` graph

Usage: kglite write [OPTIONS] <GRAPH> <QUERY>

Arguments:
  <GRAPH>  Path to the `.kgl` file
  <QUERY>  Cypher statement

Options:
      --format <FORMAT>            Output format [default: table] [possible values: table, csv, json]
      --save                       Persist the graph after a successful statement
      --write-scope <WRITE_SCOPE>  Comma-separated node-type whitelist for CREATE/SET mutations
      --git-sha <GIT_SHA>          Git SHA to stamp on auto_timestamp types
      --modified-by <MODIFIED_BY>  Actor id to stamp on auto_timestamp types
  -h, --help                       Print help
